element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary melee tool available from game start; foundational for all combat
Core movement mechanics taught immediately through level navigation
Resource management introduced early; scattered throughout each stage
Longer range than katana; can be swapped for katana; requires learning different attack patterns (diagonal/horizontal swings)
Player can hang and move under overhangs; adds vertical movement complexity
Limited supply consumable; obtained from item boxes; supplements melee combat
Limited supply consumable; area damage; obtained from item boxes
Costs ~50% max vitality; damages all on-screen enemies; risk-reward mechanic

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game (upon picking up duplicate weapons)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Attack power increments to level 3 max; weapon degrades if player takes heavy damage; encourages careful play
Stronger enemies requiring pattern recognition; teach boss mechanics before main bosses
Unique patterns; explode on defeat, healing nearby players; health carries between stages
Simultaneous play; shared continue pool; one player controls Hayate, other Kaede
Advanced meta-progression; allows wielding both katana and kusarigama simultaneously

typical_relief_triggers: Finding vitality potions in item boxes
Reaching safe platforms or rest areas
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy encounters (multiple types throughout each level)
Main boss battles at stage end
Low vitality state (below 50%, cannot use special attack)
Approaching continue limit (only 5 total, shared in co-op)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fast-paced action with escalating difficulty; tension builds through multiple enemy types, sub-bosses lead to main boss confrontations; relief provided by resource pickups and boss healing explosions

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden item boxes and secret power-ups require exploration; not all pickups visible or obvious
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Secret power-ups hidden in some levels enable dual-weapon mastery; optional progression path

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of core mechanics (katana, movement, crouching); basic enemy types; teaches resource management
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of kusarigama and projectile weapons; sub-bosses appear; weapon leveling becomes strategic
difficulty_curve.late_game: Increased enemy density; complex boss patterns; weapon degradation pressure; continued resource scarcity
notable_spikes: Final level (Level 5, only 2 stages but increased intensity)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of first four levels contains exactly 3 stages; progression through standard enemies, sub-boss mid-stage, main boss at stage end
Final level condensed to only 2 stages instead of 3; likely represents culmination against Emperor Garuda; increased difficulty density
Defeated bosses explode and heal players proportionally to proximity; encourages aggressive close-range play; health carries to next stage (until potions used or death)
Vitality potions, weapon upgrades (katana, kusarigama), projectiles (shuriken, grenades) scattered throughout stages; encourages exploration

sources_used: Wikipedia - Shadow of the Ninja (gameplay section, level structure, weapons, mechanics)
The Cutting Room Floor - Shadow of the Ninja (regional differences, level select data)
